proposed_catalog_row.Paper: Monacelli, Perotti, and Trigari (2010), Unemployment fiscal multipliers
proposed_catalog_row.Category: Fiscal policy; labor search; unemployment; sticky prices
proposed_catalog_row.KeyFeatures: Government spending shock; Mortensen-Pissarides matching; Nash bargaining; investment adjustment costs; Calvo pricing; Taylor rule; markup channel

proposed_status_row.formula_quality: baseline equations source-backed; Calvo recursions need source review
proposed_status_row.notes: Do not merge automatically without reviewing Calvo price-setting equations and implementation comments on paper equations (22) and (28).

validation.reason: User instructed not to run Dynare.
commands_to_run:
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re
model = "NK_MPT10"
base = Path("mmb-paper-derivations/derivations") / model
en = (base / f"{model}_derivation.en.md").read_text()
assert all(f"## {i}." in en for i in range(1, 9))
assert re.search(r"\(F1\)", en)
print("archive_entry_checks_ok")
PY

python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re
model = "NK_MPT10"
base = Path("mmb-paper-derivations/derivations") / model
en = (base / f"{model}_derivation.en.md").read_text()
zh = (base / f"{model}_derivation.zh.md").read_text()
assert len(re.findall(r"\(F\d+\)", en)) == len(re.findall(r"\(F\d+\)", zh))
print("f_number_counts_match")
PY
